title: Registers, Shift Registers and Counters
summary: Explains parallel registers, shift registers (classic, bidirectional, universal), and binary counters built from bistable circuits.
content_points: Parallel register: n bistable circuits sharing the same clock; all bits change simultaneously.
Shift register (flip-flops required): memorises the last n values applied on input; bits shift one position per cycle.
Universal shift register: serial/parallel inputs and outputs; left or right shift; controlled by 2-bit mode selector.
Binary counter: n-bit number increments on each clock tick; wraps from maximum back to 0.
learning_objectives: Build a parallel register from D flip-flops
Explain the operation of classic and shift registers
Describe the universal shift register mode table
Design a binary counter using the FSM approach
